message:
fix(fcp-bootstrap): verify CA bit from X.509 BasicConstraints, not metadata

Hardens provisioning certificate selection so a token identity whose
`TokenCertificate.is_ca` flag claims "true" in metadata cannot be used as a
trust anchor unless the DER-encoded certificate itself carries an
RFC 5280 BasicConstraints extension with `cA=TRUE`. The metadata flag is
driver-populated and was previously the sole gate on whether a cert could
chain-sign another, which means a compromised or misconfigured driver could
present a non-CA leaf as if it were an issuer.

Changes in crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:

- Parse each candidate's DER and require BasicConstraints.ca == true via
  the new `x509_basic_constraints_ca` helper before treating it as a
  self-signed anchor or as a chain issuer.
- `certificate_has_x509_issuer_chain` now
  (a) re-derives the CA bit for the candidate from its own parsed
      BasicConstraints rather than trusting `certificate.is_ca`, and
  (b) for each potential issuer, re-derives the CA bit *and* re-checks
      `issuer_parsed.subject() == parsed.issuer()` against the parsed
      DER, so metadata-level subject/issuer strings cannot be used to
      smuggle a non-CA leaf into the trust path.
- `select_certificate_for_provisioning` adds a pre-filter that rejects
  any identity whose own DER declares `cA=TRUE`, because a CA cert must
  never be used as the owner signing key for FCP provisioning.
- New `certificate_der_declares_ca` helper returns Option<bool>; None
  means the DER is unparseable or has trailing junk and is refused.
- Regression test
  `select_cert_non_ca_issuer_with_ca_metadata_returns_refusal` constructs
  a leaf signed by a non-CA issuer that *claims* CA status only in
  metadata and asserts the selector refuses to provision. Builds on a
  new `test_x509_leaf_signed_by_non_ca_metadata_issuer` fixture helper
  that uses rcgen to generate a real X.509 chain.

Changes in crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/soft_token.rs:

- Deduplicate PIN + token-identity validation into a private
  `validate_token_and_pin` helper and call it from
  `open_authenticated_session`, `enumerate_certificates`, and
  `enumerate_keys`. Prior to this change the certificate- and
  key-enumeration paths ignored both the PIN and the DetectedToken
  identity entirely, so a caller could enumerate soft-token material
  without ever authenticating. All three entrypoints now go through the
  same constant-time PIN comparison (via `HardwareTokenPin`) and the
  same provider/slot identity check.

Cargo.lock adds rcgen 0.14.7 + its yasna/time transitive deps to
fcp-bootstrap's [dev-dependencies] graph so the new test fixture can
mint a real X.509 issuer.

  "rand 0.8.6",

  "x25519-dalek",
@@ -10486,6 +10488,20 @@ dependencies = [
  "crossbeam-utils",
 ]
